description_long: This hardware store is a multi-story urban building with a steeply pitched roof and three dormer windows on the uppermost level. The facade features two main floors with large, arched windows and decorative molding, as well as a prominent ground-level entrance flanked by pilasters. The front elevation is detailed with classical architectural elements, including cornices and window frames, giving it a traditional and somewhat historic appearance. The sides and rear of the building are plain and unadorned, lacking windows or decorative features. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, with the roof likely made of slate or similar material. The overall form is rectangular, with a vertical emphasis and a symmetrical, formal street-facing facade.
description: A multi-story, traditional hardware store with a detailed, classical facade, arched windows, and a steep roof with dormers.
description_view_0: The front and left sides are visible, showing the ornate facade with arched windows, dormers, and the main entrance.
description_view_1: The rear and left sides are visible, both plain and featureless with no windows or decorative elements.
description_view_2: The rear and right sides are visible, both plain and lacking any architectural details.
description_view_3: The front and right sides are visible, highlighting the detailed facade, dormers, and main entrance from a slightly different angle.
attribute_storefront_layout: The storefront features a symmetrical arrangement with a central entrance at ground level, flanked by large windows and decorative pilasters. Upper floors have arched windows and a row of dormers on the steep roof, with no visible exterior displays.
